title: Air-fryer pork chops
subtitle: Bone-in chops with a crisp fat cap
excerpt: Bone-in pork chops seasoned with smoked paprika, garlic powder, and salt, cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 14 minutes. The fat cap renders and crisps against the basket wall. The result is a juicy chop with a browned, slightly firm exterior that a pan can replicate but takes more attention.
sourceNotes: Pork chops cooked in an air fryer became a popular weeknight method in Britain because the appliance produces a result closer to the high-dry-heat needed for good pork than a standard oven. Bone-in chops are preferred over boneless for the same reason as bone-in chicken: the bone conducts heat more slowly, giving the fat time to render before the interior overcooks.

content.text: Oil the meat, not the basket. Brushing oil directly onto the chop produces a better, more even colour than spraying the basket. A small amount of oil spread over the surface helps the seasoning adhere and produces a consistent brown crust rather than patchy darker spots.

Pat the {{pork-chop}} dry and brush on both sides with {{olive-oil}}. Mix the {{smoked-paprika}}, {{garlic-powder}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}} and rub into both sides. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes. Stand the chops fat-cap-side down in the basket for 2 minutes to render the fat, then lay flat. Cook at 200°C for 6 minutes on each side, totalling 14 minutes. An internal temperature of 63°C means the pork is cooked through and still moist. Rest for 3 minutes.

items.cause: Overcooked past 70°C internal, or chop too thin
items.fix: Pork is safe at 63°C and is noticeably juicier than at 70°C. Use chops at least 2.5 cm thick: thinner chops cook too fast in the air fryer to develop any juiciness

items.symptom: Fat cap rubbery rather than crispy
items.cause: Fat cap not rendered before laying flat
items.fix: The 2-minute stand on the fat edge renders out the fat under the rind. Without this step, the rind stays chewy. Score through the rind with a sharp knife before seasoning to help it crisp

items.symptom: Exterior very dark but centre still raw
items.cause: Chop too thick, or temperature too high
items.fix: Reduce temperature to 180°C and cook for 18 minutes total. Very thick chops (over 3.5 cm) benefit from a lower temperature

content.text: Air-fryer pork chops are a fast, low-mess weeknight dinner. Serve with apple sauce, mashed potato, and tenderstem broccoli. The smoked paprika and garlic rub here is a simple baseline: swap for mustard and thyme for a classic British combination, or honey and soy for an Asian-style glaze applied in the last 2 minutes.
